body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` **Triggered by:** PR #58323 by @JoshLove-msft ## What Changed PR #58323 added `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts to both `http-client-csharp` and `http-client-csharp-mgmt` packages, and integrated doc regeneration into `Generate.ps1`. As part of this, the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` was auto-regenerated using `tspd doc`. However, the `tspd` tool only regenerates emitter option documentation — it does not preserve or generate the human-authored `## Usage` section. ## Gaps Found - The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is missing the `## Usage` section (with `### Prerequisite` and `### Customizing Generated Code` sub-sections) that exists in the parallel `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` - The Prerequisites sub-section (Node.js and .NET SDK version requirements) is absent, leaving mgmt emitter users without setup guidance - There is no link to a Customization Guide for mgmt-specific generated code (comparable to the one in the data-plane README) - Neither README documents the new `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts — developers modifying emitter options manually need to know they can run `npm run regen-docs` to regenerate the README
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` **Edit** — Insert the following `## Usage` section after the `## Install` section and before the `## Emitter usage` section: ```markdown ## Usage ### Prerequisite - Install [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/download/) 20 or above. (Verify by running `node --version`) - Install [**.NET 10.0 SDK**](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/10.0) for your specific platform. (or a higher version within the 10.0.\* band) ### Customizing Generated Code For detailed instructions on how to customize the generated C# code for Azure management libraries, see the [Customization Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md). ### Regenerating Documentation The emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run: ```bash npm run regen-docs ``` This rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter). ``` #### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` **Edit** — Add a "Regenerating Documentation" sub-section under `## Usage`, after `### Customizing Generated Code`: ```markdown ### Regenerating Documentation The emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run: ```bash npm run regen-docs ``` This rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter). ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Not applicable — these are tool/generator package READMEs, not Azure SDK client library packages. No snippet test files apply. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` should contain these sections after the fix: 1. `## Install` ✅ (already present) 2. `## Usage` ❌ **missing** — add it - `### Prerequisite` - `### Customizing Generated Code` - `### Regenerating Documentation` 3. `## Emitter usage` ✅ (already present) 4. `## Emitter options` ✅ (already present, auto-generated) Reference: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order from the repository root: 1. Verify the README changes look correct manually — no build is needed for pure documentation edits to these generator packages 2. Optionally, to confirm `regen-docs` still works after the edit (note: `tspd` only regenerates the `## Emitter options` section, so the `## Usage` section added manually will be preserved): ```powershell cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt npm install npm run regen-docs ``` Check that the `## Usage` section is still present in the regenerated file (it should be, since `tspd` only affects the emitter options sections it generates).
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Azure Management Generator] Docs: Missing Usage/Prerequisites section in http-client-csharp-mgmt README
